description: Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner claimed that he came into possess an immovable property under a sale agreement pending the final transfer deeds; however, the 6th Respondent challenged that position because the vendor to the sales agreement did not have title to the land in suit. When the 6th Respondent lost possession, he made a complaint to the police and filed a private plaint in the Magistrate’s Court under Section 66 of the Primary Court Procedure Act; both proceedings were challenged by the Petitioner. Held – Application dismissed because the Petitioner could have invoked alternative remedies and the Magistrate’s Court acted within the law in both proceedings.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
